target.name: Hugging Face
target.founder: Clément Delangue, Julien Chaumond, Thomas Wolf

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series D → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.5x) to $4.5B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same-stage peers in AI & Software. Range: $2.0B - $29.3B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in AI & Compute

private.name: Anysphere
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series D), post-money $29.3B

private.name: Wayve
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series D), post-money $8.6B

private.name: Cohere
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series D), post-money $7B

private.name: Hive AI
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series D), post-money $2B

data.narrative:
Hugging Face is a Series D AI & Software company currently valued at $4.5B post-money following $400M in total capital raised. The valuation analysis draws from a peer set of 4 same-stage private companies in AI & Software, alongside 1 public comparable (Palantir), supplemented by additional private frontier-tech benchmarks.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis applies a 1.5x step-up multiplier typical of Series D advancement, yielding $6.8B. Peer-median valuation across the 4-company comparable set (Anysphere at $29.3B, Wayve at $8.6B, Cohere at $7.0B, and Hive AI at $2.0B) produces $7.8B, though the wide range ($2.0B–$29.3B) reflects significant dispersion in stage-comparable valuations. The peer-median method carries elevated uncertainty given the small sample and marked heterogeneity within the AI software cohort.

The triangulated valuation range is $5.7B (low) to $9.0B (high), with a median of $7.3B. This implies modest upside from the current $4.5B valuation. Key uncertainties include the relevance of the peer set given the wide valuation dispersion, particularly the outsized $29.3B Anysphere valuation, and limited financial visibility into comparable-company revenue and growth metrics. Refinement would benefit from larger peer samples and revenue-based cross-checks.
